title: Why does buildah commit replace replace bin/ with usr/bin/, losing contents of bin/
body:
I'm trying to use `buildah from scratch` to create a new rhel8 container image. I found that the resulting container had no `dnf` or `yum`, but I'm actually trying to create a VM-like container for use in WSL, so I need those.

After digging a bit, I found that, although before committing, there is a bin/ directory containing `dnf`, after running
```
buildah commit working-container localhost/myrhel8:latest
```
the bin/ directory had been removed and replaced by a symlink to usr/bin/. Additionally, `lib -> usr/lib`, `lib64 -> usr/lib64`, and `sbin -> usr/sbin`.

1. Is this what is supposed to happen, and why?
2. What should I be doing to get what I am looking for?
3. Is this something that can be clarified in the documentation?

comments.body:
I might have figured this out?

I went through the process again, listing the target contents along the way, and I see that the only reason the bin/ directory existed there at all prior to commit is because they were added during a package installation. That doesn't completely explain why `buildah` creates those symlinks, but at least I know that if I create the symlinks myself before installing any packages, then the files will be installed into the locations that remain present in the final image.
@stevecj I am not entirely sure if buildah itself is doing it, could you please share a minimal reproducer ?
I am pretty sure this is nothing to do with Buildah.

If it has nothing to do with Buildah itself, then it must be in something that rhel8 does that Buildah invokes during commit. Right? Does Buildah run anything during the commit that we don't explicitly specify?
Buildah is using golang tar on the file system on COW file systems.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we have not heard any other feedback, closing.
